title: Jhara Ecosystem Signature
description: Schema for community-contributed ecosystem signatures. Place JSON files matching this schema in ~/Library/Application Support/com.hmyousuf.jhara.app/signatures/ to add detection for tools not included in the built-in database.

filename.description: The filename to detect at the project root. Use '*.ext' for wildcard suffix matching (e.g., '*.tf' matches any .tf file).

ecosystem.description: Identifier for the ecosystem. Use snake_case. This becomes the label in the UI.

artifact_paths.description: The directories to clean when this project type is detected.

relative_path.description: Path relative to the project root (or relative to $HOME if is_global is true).

safety_tier.description: How safely this artifact can be deleted. Use 'safe' for anything automatically regenerated by the build tool. Use 'caution' for expensive-to-rebuild or historically-valuable artifacts. Use 'risky' for live infrastructure state. Use 'blocked' for things that must NEVER be deleted (credentials, state files).

is_global.description: Set to true for global caches in the user's home directory rather than inside a project root.

recovery_command.description: The command a developer would run to regenerate this artifact after deletion. Shown in the confirmation UI.

typical_size_mb_min.description: Typical minimum size of this artifact in megabytes.

typical_size_mb_max.description: Typical maximum size of this artifact in megabytes.

content_key.description: Optional string that must appear in the detected file's content to confirm the match. Useful for disambiguating files that share a name across ecosystems (e.g., a generic build.gradle vs a Kotlin build.gradle.kts).

priority.description: Priority for disambiguation when multiple signatures match the same directory. Higher priority wins. The built-in database uses 0 for base signatures and 10+ for more specific ones.

stale_threshold_days.description: Number of days of inactivity before this project is considered stale and eligible for automated cleanup.
